user_query:
You're our ASO specialist for a new productivity app called FocusFlow. Using the inputs under input/, produce a deterministic ASO plan and store listings for both iOS and Google Play. Read all files from input/ and write all results to output/ using the exact filenames below.

Inputs to read:
- input/keywords.csv — candidate keywords with metrics
- input/negative_keywords.txt — exact-match keywords to exclude
- input/brand_terms.txt — brand names that must not appear in any listing text
- input/app_brand.txt — the app brand name to use (expected: FocusFlow)
- input/translations.json — Spanish (es-ES) translations for keywords
- input/reviews.jsonl — user review items with issue_tag and platform for aggregation

Task requirements:
1) Keyword selection (for iOS and Google Play):
- Compute opportunity_score for each keyword exactly as: volume * (1 - difficulty) * relevance.
- Exclude any keyword that exactly matches a term in input/negative_keywords.txt.
- Build two sets of rankings:
  a) iOS: include keywords whose platform is "ios" or "both".
  b) Google Play: include keywords whose platform is "gp" or "both".
- For each platform, select the top 8 keywords by opportunity_score descending.
- Ties in score must be broken by keyword in ascending alphabetical order.
- Preserve the keyword casing exactly as in input.
- Round opportunity_score values to 2 decimal places in your outputs.

2) Write output/aso_plan.json with:
- formula: the exact string "opportunity_score=volume*(1-difficulty)*relevance".
- excluded_keywords: the list of excluded keywords that were present in the CSV (preserve original casing), sorted alphabetically.
- ios.selected_keywords: an array of 8 objects with fields keyword and opportunity_score (rounded to 2 decimals) in ranked order.
- gp.selected_keywords: the same for Google Play.
- ios.top3 and gp.top3 arrays listing the top 3 keywords (strings) in ranked order.
- ios.top5 and gp.top5 arrays listing the top 5 keywords (strings) in ranked order.

3) iOS store listing: write output/ios_listing.json with fields:
- title: must start with the brand name from input/app_brand.txt, be at most 30 characters, and include exactly one of the iOS top 3 keywords.
- subtitle: at most 30 characters and include a different iOS top 3 keyword than used in the title.
- keywords: a single comma-separated string (no spaces after commas) containing exactly the 8 selected iOS keywords in ranked order; total length must be <= 100 characters.
- None of title, subtitle, or keywords may contain any term from input/brand_terms.txt. The brand from app_brand.txt may be used.

4) Google Play listing: write output/gp_listing.json with fields:
- title: must start with the brand name from input/app_brand.txt, be at most 30 characters, and include exactly one of the GP top 3 keywords.
- short_description: <= 80 characters and include a different GP top 3 keyword than the one used in the title.
- long_description: between 250 and 400 characters (inclusive). It must include each of the GP top 5 keywords at least once and no more than 3 times each. It must not contain any term from input/brand_terms.txt. The brand from app_brand.txt may be used.
- features: an array of exactly 5 bullet items (strings), each 15–60 characters, each containing at least one of the 8 selected GP keywords. None may contain any term from input/brand_terms.txt.

5) Localization keywords: write output/localization.json with this structure:
{
  "en-US": {"ios_keywords": [...], "gp_keywords": [...]},
  "es-ES": {"ios_keywords": [...], "gp_keywords": [...]} 
}
- en-US arrays must list the selected keywords for each platform in the same ranked order.
- es-ES arrays must be the Spanish translations for those keywords using input/translations.json for exact string mappings; if a mapping is missing, keep the English term.

6) Reviews aggregation: write output/reviews_report.json with:
- total_reviews: total count of lines in input/reviews.jsonl.
- by_platform: an object with keys ios and gp and their respective counts.
- top_issues: an array of the top 3 issue_tag values across all reviews, sorted by frequency descending (ties broken by issue_tag ascending), each item with fields issue_tag and count.

Global constraints:
- Do not include any term from input/brand_terms.txt in any output text. The app brand from input/app_brand.txt must be used as instructed for titles.
- Use only the data provided in input/ for all computations and translations.
- Ensure all JSON you write is valid and UTF-8 encoded.

Deliverables to create:
- output/aso_plan.json
- output/ios_listing.json
- output/gp_listing.json
- output/localization.json
- output/reviews_report.json
